acceptance_targets: [ch16-craft-01] PASS if each cited span contains at least one concrete, observable crowd action or material moss change where the current draft explains the joke or the mechanism, and if a reader can infer the chorus tactic's success without narrator sentences that tell us the crowd loved a line because it was funny/true or that laughter is what carries it home. FAIL if the revised passage still translates the comedy or the victory into thesis statements.

[ch16-prose-01] PASS if every cited span is rewritten as grammatically complete narration and the revised chapter contains no more than two negative-correction fragment sequences of the 'Not X. Not Y.' type. FAIL if three or more subjectless corrective fragments remain in narration, or if lines like 'Not hard,' 'Not purity,' or 'Not victory music' survive in essentially the same clipped form.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each cited span contains at least one concrete, observable crowd action or material moss change where the current draft explains the joke or the mechanism, and if a reader can infer the chorus tactic's success without narrator sentences that tell us the crowd loved a line because it was funny/true or that laughter is what carries it home. FAIL if the revised passage still translates the comedy or the victory into thesis statements.
findings.problem: The narration keeps stepping in to explain how the chorus tactic works, why the crowd is laughing, and what the public victory means. In a comedy-driven confrontation chapter, that interpretive gloss deflates the joke and weakens the scene's trust in observable public behavior.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:63-67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:183-185, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:243-245, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:383-385, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:415, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:437-439, replace explanatory summary with concrete aftershocks: people misreading and correcting, bodies stopping in traffic, duplicate strips going up, or visible moss response on specific bridge sections. Keep at most one explicit statement of the read-say-repeat mechanism in the chapter and let the later Bellglass beats demonstrate the rest through action.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if every cited span is rewritten as grammatically complete narration and the revised chapter contains no more than two negative-correction fragment sequences of the 'Not X. Not Y.' type. FAIL if three or more subjectless corrective fragments remain in narration, or if lines like 'Not hard,' 'Not purity,' or 'Not victory music' survive in essentially the same clipped form.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into clipped negative-correction fragments such as 'Not hard. Not intimate either.' and 'Not purity. Not the clean replacement...'. Because the constitution forbids elliptical clipping in narration, and because this construction recurs across multiple key beats, it reads as autopilot rather than controlled rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the cited narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:127-128, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:239-240, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:297, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:367, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:401-403, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:415 into fully grammatical sentences with explicit subjects and verbs. Preserve snap where needed, but vary the cadence and let concrete nouns, sensory detail, or clean declarative syntax do the work instead of repeating the 'Not X. Not Y.' correction move.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
